source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Default Network Does Not Exist in a Project

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Default Network Does Not Exist in a Project
input_row.description: To prevent use of default network, a project should not have a default network.
input_row.rationale: The default network has a preconfigured network configuration and automatically generates the following insecure firewall rules: • default-allow-internal: Allows ingress connections for all protocols and ports among instances in the network. • default-allow-ssh: Allows ingress connections on TCP port 22(SSH) from any source to any instance in the network. • default-allow-rdp: Allows ingress connections on TCP port 3389(RDP) from any source to any instance in the network. • default-allow-icmp: Allows ingress ICMP traffic from any source to any instance in the network. These automatically created firewall rules do not get audit logged by default. Furthermore, the default network is an auto mode network, which means that its subnets use the same predefined range of IP addresses, and as a result, it's not possible to use Cloud VPN or VPC Network Peering with the default network. Based on organization security and networking requirements, the organization should create a new network and delete the default network. Impact: When an organization deletes the default network, it will need to remove all asests from that network and migrate them to a new network.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VPC networks page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/networking/networks/list. 2. Ensure that a network with the name default is not present. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Set the project name in the Google Cloud Shell: gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID 2. List the networks configured in that project: gcloud compute networks list It should not list default as one of the available networks in that project.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VPC networks page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/networking/networks/list. 2. Click the network named default. 3. On the network detail page, click EDIT. 4. Click DELETE VPC NETWORK. 5. If needed, create a new network to replace the default network. From Google Cloud CLI For each Google Cloud Platform project, 1. Delete the default network: gcloud compute networks delete default 2. If needed, create a new network to replace it: gcloud compute networks create NETWORK_NAME Prevention: The user can prevent the default network and its insecure default firewall rules from being created by setting up an Organization Policy to Skip default network creation at https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/compute- skipDefaultNetworkCreation. Default Value: By default, for each project, a default network is created. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/networking#firewall_rules 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/latest/networks/insert 3. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/latest/networks/delete 4. https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/firewall-rules-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/vpc#default-network 6. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/compute/networks/delete
input_row.impact: When an organization deletes the default network, it will need to remove all asests from that network and migrate them to a new network.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control can be entirely verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The audit instructions included a specific gcloud command to list networks and check for the existence of a network named "default", indicating that this property is accessible and can be automated using GCP CLI. 

- **Can GCP APIs query this property?** Yes, the default network is a standard GCP resource that can be queried using GCP APIs.
- **Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)?** Yes, it involves network resource configurations.
- **Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this?** Yes, the `gcloud compute networks list` command is appropriate for this check.
- **Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this?** Yes, the command `gcloud compute networks list` can be used to verify the existence of the default network.
- **Does it require SSH/RDP to instances?** No, this is an infrastructure-level check, not dependent on individual instances.
- **Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands?** Yes, it specifically mentions `gcloud compute networks list`.

Given these points, the compliance control can be checked programmatically, thus categorized as Automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_compute_default_network_absence

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the command `gcloud compute networks list` to enumerate all networks in the specified GCP project.
- **Configuration Check:** Verify that the list of networks does not include a network named "default".
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** If the "default" network is not present in the output list.
  - **Fail:** If the "default" network is listed in the output.
- **Process:**
  1. Set the correct project in gcloud configuration: `gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID`
  2. Execute the command to list networks: `gcloud compute networks list`
  3. Analyze the output to ensure "default" is not among the networks listed.
- **Expected Output:** Verification that no network named "default" exists in the list, confirming compliance with the control.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
